texto: When you're no one from nowhere it's best to know your limits. Rich kids can play at bohemia, but wealth has long tendrils; it twines into a safety net which can also be a trap for the unprepared. Rich kids have families and backgrounds and connections, and they ask questions, because their world functions on being able to place people. I couldn't expose myself to that.
explicacao: Citação de L.S. Hilton sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “When you're no one from nowhere it's best to know your limits. Rich kids can play at bohemia, but…” articula reflexao, wealth, nowhere em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e reflexao.

texto: Wealth creeps under your epidermis like poison. It invades your posture, your gestures, the way you carry yourself.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a L.S. Hilton: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Wealth creeps under your epidermis like poison. texto: Choices are made before explanations, whether or not we care to know it.
